#34950 Cleanup nano refetch commands in the background (#42472)

<!-- Add the related story/sub-task/bug number, like Resolves #123, or
remove if NA -->
**Related issue:** Resolves #34950

I changed from the original spec of 100 old commands to 3 due to load
test results. Admittedly my load test meant a very large number of hosts
all checked in and triggered deletion at once but at 100 per host and
per command the load was too high. 3 still results in cleanup over time
and doesn't seem to cause load issues.

# Checklist for submitter

If some of the following don't apply, delete the relevant line.

- [x] Changes file added for user-visible changes in `changes/`,
`orbit/changes/` or `ee/fleetd-chrome/changes`.
See [Changes
files](https://github.com/fleetdm/fleet/blob/main/docs/Contributing/guides/committing-changes.md#changes-files)
for more information.

- [x] Input data is properly validated, `SELECT *` is avoided, SQL
injection is prevented (using placeholders for values in statements), JS
inline code is prevented especially for url redirects, and untrusted
data interpolated into shell scripts/commands is validated against shell
metacharacters.
- [x] If paths of existing endpoints are modified without backwards
compatibility, checked the frontend/CLI for any necessary changes

## Testing

- [x] Added/updated automated tests
- [x] Where appropriate, [automated tests simulate multiple hosts and
test for host
isolation](https://github.com/fleetdm/fleet/blob/main/docs/Contributing/reference/patterns-backend.md#unit-testing)
(updates to one hosts's records do not affect another)

- [x] QA'd all new/changed functionality manually

commit.message:
Fix BUG-012: Enhance AI prompts for stunning, animated UIs

**Animation & Visual Enhancements (MANDATORY):**
- Add explicit requirement for stunning, sleek, modern designs
- Enforce framer-motion animations for all React/Next.js projects
- Add gradient text, glass effects, decorative orbs to all designs
- Add micro-interactions on all clickable elements
- Add hover effects with shadows and transforms

**Dependencies Added:**
- framer-motion ^11.0.0 for React and Next.js projects
- lucide-react ^0.321.0 for beautiful icons

**Design Rules Strengthened:**
- NO static, boring UIs allowed
- EVERY page must have entrance animations
- ALL buttons need hover effects with shadows
- Cards must have scale and shadow on hover
- Forms need focus states with transitions

This should result in significantly more polished and animated generated UIs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix BUG-011: Dynamic page titles and BUG-010: Next.js import paths

- Add useEffect to update document.title in GeneratePage and ProjectDetailPage
- Page titles now show "{ProjectName} - NexaStudio" instead of static "NexaStudio"
- Add explicit import path rules to Next.js prompt template

- Use UTF-8 bytes directly instead of double Base64 encode/decode
- Pad key to minimum 32 bytes for HS256 security

- Implement 401 retry with refresh token before logout
- Store and clear refresh token in store.ts
- Don't auto-logout on 403 (let UI handle)

- Check for null user/userId before ownership comparison

Merge PR #67: fix CI hang and datetime test failures

Fixes persistent CI job cancellation (19-min hang after tests complete)
and 6 TestDatetimeInjection test failures.

Root causes fixed:
1. pytest_sessionfinish duplicate definition — Python used last definition,
   so os._exit() never ran. Merged into single function.
2. Background threads (JobRunner ThreadPoolExecutor) blocked on outbound
   HTTP kept process alive after tests; os._exit() now hard-exits cleanly.
3. unified_agent.run() yields a planning THOUGHT before the LLM call;
   tests using 'for _ in ...: break' captured only that step so
   generate_content.call_args was always None. Fixed with list(agent.run()).
4. Module-level setdefault('GEMINI_API_KEY', ...) in test files re-injected
   invalid key during collection; cleared per-test in autouse fixture.

commit.message:
fix: correct Dockerfile ARG scope comment and harden Renovate caddy builder customManager

* Initial plan

* fix: correct Dockerfile ARG comment and improve Renovate caddy builder customManager

- Fix inaccurate Dockerfile comment that incorrectly stated ARGs before the
  first FROM cannot reference the ARG in FROM lines. ARGs declared before
  the first FROM ARE available to FROM lines; they are only NOT in scope
  inside build stage bodies (RUN, COPY, etc.).
- Harden the FROM line by hardcoding the version ('2.11.2-builder') instead
  of using the ${CADDY_VERSION} ARG reference. With SHA pinning (@sha256:),
  the ARG reference in the tag was cosmetic only; Docker always pulls by
  digest. Hardcoding enables the Renovate fix below.
- Update Renovate customManager to capture currentValue from the FROM tag
  ('2.11.2-builder') instead of the ARG line. This allows Renovate to:
  - Use extractVersionTemplate to strip '-builder' for semver comparison
  - Look up the manifest for 'caddy:X.Y.Z-builder' (not 'caddy:X.Y.Z')
    to get the correct builder-specific digest
  - Use autoReplaceStringTemplate to atomically update ARG CADDY_VERSION,
    the FROM version tag, and the SHA digest in a single Renovate PR

All other findings from the review were already correctly implemented:
all action SHAs verified, cosign/scout SHA-256 values verified, Alpine 3.23.3
and caddy:2.11.2-builder digests confirmed correct, docker_proxy module name
confirmed, GHCR auth ordering correct, and all other workflow safeguards present.

commit.message:
Add wrapper tasks: per-subtask caching for orchestrator functions

Wrapper tasks (e.g. load_redcap) call multiple lower-level tasks that
should be individually cached. Mark a task with wrapper: true in
kptn.yaml and kptn will:

- AST-scan the wrapper function body to discover subtask calls
- Evaluate each subtask's cache independently with cascade invalidation
- Execute via monkey-patching: skip cached subtasks, run changed ones
- Record per-subtask cache entries as {wrapper}.{subtask} keys

New files:
- kptn/caching/wrapper.py: AST-based subtask discovery
- tests/test_wrapper_tasks.py: 20 tests (unit + integration)

Modified:
- kptn-schema.json: wrapper boolean property
- TaskStateCache: wrapper evaluation and subtask cache methods
- vanilla.py: run_wrapper_task() with caching proxies
- cli/task_validation.py: wrapper config validation
- cli/__init__.py: wire validation into validate command

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: publish web-components to npm and fix Docker nightly version source (#1175)

Two issues broke the Docker nightly build. (1) web-components was
missing versionPolicyName in rush.json so Rush never published it to
npm. Since CLI depends on it transitively
(cli->server->web-server->web->web-components), npm install fails with a
404. Fix: add versionPolicyName grackle. (2) docker-nightly.yml resolved
the version via npm view which returns stale versions. Fix: read from
version-policies.json instead.
